claims.claim: Dell'Oro counts roughly 40 vendors now competing in the coolant distribution unit (CDU) segment of the liquid-cooling market
claims.sourceTitle: Market at the Boiling Point: Is the CDU Space Becoming Saturated? - Dell'Oro Group
claims.excerpt: With around 40 vendors rushing into coolant distribution units, liquid cooling is surging—but how many players can the market sustain?

claims.claim: Dell'Oro reports the Direct Liquid Cooling (DLC) market grew 156% year-over-year in Q2 2025
claims.sourceTitle: Market at the Boiling Point: Is the CDU Space Becoming Saturated? - Dell'Oro Group
claims.excerpt: the Data Center Direct Liquid Cooling (DLC) market surged 156 percent year-over-year in 2Q 2025 and is projected to reach close to $6 billion by 2029

claims.claim: Dell'Oro projects the DLC market to reach close to $6 billion by 2029
claims.sourceTitle: Market at the Boiling Point: Is the CDU Space Becoming Saturated? - Dell'Oro Group
claims.excerpt: projected to reach close to $6 billion by 2029, fueled by the relentless growth of accelerated computing workloads

claims.claim: NVIDIA states its 800 VDC power architecture transition for 1MW+ racks begins in 2027
claims.sourceTitle: NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
claims.excerpt: NVIDIA is leading the transition to 800 VDC data center power infrastructure to support 1 MW IT racks and beyond, starting in 2027.

claims.claim: NVIDIA's 800VDC architecture is projected to cut total cost of ownership by up to 30% and maintenance costs by up to 70%
claims.sourceTitle: NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
claims.excerpt: improve end-to-end efficiency by up to 5%, reduce maintenance costs by up to 70%, and lower cooling expenses, enabling sustainable growth and cutting total cost of ownership by up to 30%

claims.claim: Specific vendors achieved NVIDIA RVL qualification status for CDU and SSD components in 2024, illustrating the AVL/RVL gating mechanism
claims.excerpt: in 2024, vendors like LITEON achieved RVL status for high-capacity in-row CDU liquid cooling systems designed to support NVIDIA's accelerated computing demands, while Micron Technology qualified its 9550 PCIe Gen5 SSDs for the RVL on the same platform

claims.claim: Industry analysis says self-driven B2B buyer journeys before sales contact have grown from ~57% to over 90% of the purchase process
claims.sourceTitle: Stalled at the Gatekeeper: The Hidden Friction in Complex Data Center Sales
claims.excerpt: For decades, commercial teams anchored their strategies on a classic piece of CEB/Gartner data: the idea that B2B buyers complete roughly 57% of their journey before contacting sales... that self-driven journey has blown past 90%.

claims.claim: Wiwynn, an ODM selling racks directly to hyperscalers, reports doubling revenue alongside negative cash flow — illustrating the direct-to-hyperscaler sales model's economics
claims.sourceTitle: [6669] Wiwynn: The Heavy Metal Behind AI - Silba
claims.excerpt: Revenue is doubling, cash flow is negative. The brutal economics of the ODM Direct model

claims.claim: Over 20 industry partners are aligning with NVIDIA's Vera Rubin/Kyber ecosystem ahead of the 1MW/800VDC rack transition, indicating a broad supplier field competing for early qualification slots
claims.sourceTitle: Nvidia prepares data center industry for 1MW racks and 800-volt DC power architectures - DCD
claims.excerpt: Nvidia has announced more than a dozen partners as it looks to prepare the data center industry for 800-volt DC power architectures and rack densities of 1MW.

tensions.text: Dell'Oro's count of ~40 vendors competing for CDU/rack-power business implies a naive equal-share benchmark of ~2.5% per entrant, which sits near the top of obtainableFactor's 0.5–3% band rather than validating the 1% point estimate — suggesting the true first-year capture for a new entrant amid this crowding could plausibly be lower than 1% if incumbents capture disproportionate share, contradicting the node's anchor toward 1%.

The reported 156% YoY growth of the DLC/liquid-cooling SAM and NVIDIA's 2027 architecture shift mean the SAM denominator itself is moving rapidly; a static 1% 'Year-1 obtainable share' assumption does not account for whether Year 1 is measured against a rapidly inflating or still-nascent SAM, which the node's derivation does not address.

The finding that B2B buyer journeys are now ~90% self-driven before sales contact (versus historical 57%) reinforces the node's cross-check that DC electrical gear is locked 12–24 months pre-fit-out, but pushes toward the lower end of the sensitivity band (0.5%) rather than the corrected 1% central estimate, since even more of the addressable decision is settled before a new entrant's sales motion can influence it.

absences: No documented first-year market-capture percentages for rack-PDU/CDU hardware entrants selling directly to hyperscale/large operators through NVIDIA AVL/RVL qualification were found — the searches returned qualification-process descriptions (RVL/AVL mechanics) but no share-of-market outcome data.
No source quantified the actual duration (in months) of the AVL/RVL qualification gate itself, leaving the node's '12–24mo lock-in before fit-out' cross-check uncorroborated by this search pass.

No comparable-entrant case study (e.g., a named rack-PDU or CDU startup's actual Year-1 revenue divided by SAM) was located; existing examples (Wiwynn, SuperX, FuriosaAI, Crusoe) describe scale, funding, or shipment milestones but not first-year SAM-share figures.

The search did not surface any benchmark dataset explicitly measuring 'first-year market capture for comparable DC-hardware entrants selling direct to large operators' as requested by the query intent — the closest analogues (CDU vendor count, DLC market growth rate) are market-level, not entrant-level, statistics.
